title: alsinopsis palustris
intro: Alsinopsis palustris, also known as marsh alsin, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caprifoliaceae family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is commonly found in damp environments, such as marshes and wet meadows. The medicinal properties of Alsinopsis palustris include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural antiseptic, which can be achieved through herbal preparations such as infusions and tinctures.
